assertions.title: Local API health and auth bootstrap are machine-usable
assertions.notes: Health is public, auth works, protected routes require Bearer token

assertions.title: Request ID correlation works on representative API responses
assertions.notes: Request ID middleware works on both public and protected routes

assertions.title: Request ID behavior is usable cross-origin

steps.observed: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *, Allow-Headers includes X-Request-ID, Expose-Headers includes X-Request-ID

evidence.curlOutput: CORS headers allow X-Request-ID for cross-origin clients

assertions.notes: CORS is configured to allow cross-origin request ID correlation

assertions.title: WebSocket lifecycle events are schema-stable for job monitoring

steps.observed: job.started, job.progress, job.completed, job.failed, job.cancelled all documented with stable fields
All 5 event types broadcast with type, jobId, timestamp, status fields

assertions.notes: WebSocket events are documented and implementation matches docs

assertions.title: Cancelation events are not ambiguous to clients

steps.observed: job.cancelled broadcast on DELETE /jobs/{id} with status and progress
Lines 227, 515 in server.go handle terminal state

assertions.title: Failed jobs preserve meaningful terminal state for clients

steps.observed: job.failed event includes error field, job status set to 'failed'

assertions.notes: Failed jobs retain status and error for polling clients

assertions.title: OpenClaw-facing config and manifest surfaces stay in sync

steps.observed: Valid JSON with CLI and API contracts
All properties documented in manifest match config file

assertions.notes: Manifest is in sync with implementation

summary: Tested 7 assertions: all passed. Health, auth, request ID, CORS, WebSocket lifecycle all work correctly.
